fields.description:
# Live-store remediation: drive fsck to zero (safe, reversible, batched)

## Why
`rebar fsck` on the live store = 2422 SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT + 11 ORPHAN_EVENT + 1 MISSING_CREATE. Fix #1
stopped new corruption; this cleans the backlog. DESTRUCTIVE on live shared data — safety is first-class.
NOTE the sibling deliverables this consumes are NEW work, not existing code: A1 (tricolour-head-ratfish)
adds the `.retired` rename + `RETIRED_SUFFIX`; A2 (glandulous-potent-waxwing) adds the `include_retired`
rebuild path in `reducer/_api.py`. depends_on A1 + A2 (wired).

## Repair per fault class
- SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT (2422): fault = a folded source file STILL PRESENT (listed in `source_event_uuids`
  but never deleted). Repair = retire (`.retired`, via A1) each still-present listed source. NOT a rebuild.
- ORPHAN_EVENT (11): a pre-snapshot event absent from `source_event_uuids` -> A2's rebuild path
  (`reduce_ticket(..., include_retired=True)` -> re-write snapshot). 
- MISSING_CREATE (1): human triage.

## Safety
- Pre-remediation backup: `git -C <tracker> tag pre-a3-remediation <oid>` (+ optional `rebar export`) BEFORE
  any write. Rollback: `git -C <tracker> reset --hard pre-a3-remediation && git push --force-with-lease`.
  Because concurrent user writes during the window would be lost by a hard reset, the run is gated on the
  reconciler+writers being paused (below); document that manual writers must hold off during the window.
- Dry-run FIRST: `fsck --repair --dry-run` prints per-ticket before/after diff + dispositions, writes NOTHING;
  the live run is gated on human review of that diff.
- Reconciler pause (REAL mechanism — `OBSERVE` is NOT a Mode; `mode.py` has exactly reconcile-check/dry-run/
  bootstrap-strict/bootstrap-throttle/live): pause by (a) disabling the `reconcile-bridge.yml` GHA schedule for
  the window, or (b) holding the reconciler pass-lock `refs/reconciler/lock` (ADR 0031) for the duration; verify
  no pass is mid-flight before starting; failsafe: release/re-enable after N minutes or on repair-process exit.
- Idempotent + resumable: a per-ticket repaired marker = a side-file `<ticket_dir>/.a3-repaired` written after
  that ticket's atomic per-snapshot rewrite; a re-run skips tickets whose marker exists; markers are swept once
  the whole store reaches zero faults. Per-ticket failure = log + skip + include in the final report; the batch
  does NOT abort. Replacing a bad SNAPSHOT is atomic (write `<snap>.tmp`, then `os.rename` over the old).
- Batched/throttled: `fsck --repair --limit=200`, wait >=30s between batches.

## Orphan disposition policy
- AUTO-RECOVER (additive): COMMENT, LINK, UNLINK, TAG_DELTA, COMMITS.
- HUMAN TRIAGE (LWW-scalar; correct order unrecoverable): STATUS, EDIT (title/description/priority/assignee/parent),
  FILE_IMPACT, VERIFY_COMMANDS, SIGNATURE-mirror. Decision recorded in the PR / a linked ops doc.

## Scope
`_commands/fsck.py` (`--repair`/`--dry-run`/`--limit` + per-ticket marker + per-snapshot rewrite),
`_commands/compact.py` (retire-still-present path). Consumes A1's `.retired` + A2's `include_retired`. Gated on
RC1 fix #1 (deployed).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `fsck --repair --dry-run` prints per-ticket before/after + dispositions, writes nothing; live run gated on review.
- [ ] Pre-remediation backup tag created; rollback documented AND rehearsed on a fixture.
- [ ] Reconciler paused via GHA-schedule-disable OR pass-lock hold (verified no in-flight pass) with failsafe auto-resume; manual writers held off during the window.
- [ ] Idempotent/resumable via per-ticket `.a3-repaired` markers (swept at the end); per-ticket failure logs+skips (no batch abort); per-snapshot replace is atomic; batched <=200.
- [ ] Synthetic-fixture unit tests: known SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT / ORPHAN_EVENT / MISSING_CREATE (incl. `.retired`) repair correctly.
- [ ] Live store reaches 0 UNRESOLVED faults (triaged+documented orphans count as resolved); before/after counts recorded.
